rcodialog1_text: Well, well... what brings you to my humble camp, stranger? You got the look of someone who ain't afraid of bendin' a law or two...
rcodialog1_button1: I heard you know about moonshine.
rcodialog2_text: *chuckles* Moonshine, eh? You got good taste, friend. I've been brewin' the finest shine in these parts for near 40 years. Learned from my pappy, who learned from his.
rcodialog3_text: Teach ya? *spits* Boy, this ain't some Sunday school lesson. Moonshinin' is an ART. Takes patience, skill, and a nose for what works. But... I got a proposition for ya.
rcodialog4_text: I'll sell ya the tools to get started - a basic still, ingredients, the works. But I ain't sharin' my recipes for free. You gotta EARN that knowledge, partner.
rcodialog5_text: Simple. You experiment, find recipes on your own. Corn, sugar, water, yeast - them's the basics. Mix 'em right, you get good shine. Mix 'em wrong... well, you'll know. Come back when you've discovered at least THREE recipes, and we'll talk business proper.
rcodialog5_button1: Deal. What do you have for sale?
rcodialog5_button2: I need to think about it.
rcodialog6_text: Suit yourself, stranger. But if you change your mind and want to make some real money... you know where to find me.
rcodialog7_text: *waves hand dismissively* Then get outta here. I don't got time for lazy folk. This business is for people with GRIT.
rcodialog8_text: Back so soon? You discovered %d recipes so far... but I need you to discover %d more before we talk real business. Keep experimentin', partner.
rcodialog9_text: Alright, alright... I'll give ya a nudge. Start simple - corn, water, sugar, yeast. That's your foundation. Want somethin' fancier? Try addin' fruit, honey, or herbs. But don't go throwin' random junk in there, or you'll poison yourself!
rcodialog10_text: *grins* Well I'll be damned! You actually did it! You got the nose for this business, friend. From now on, you're welcome to my full stock.
rcodialog10_text2: And listen... I think you're ready. There's someone I want you to meet.
rcodialog11_text: *tips hat with a knowing grin* Well well, if it ain't my favorite shiner! Hope Marcus is treatin' you right over there. Man's got a keen eye for talent, that's for damn sure. What can Old Coot do for ya today?
rcodialog12_text: *lowers voice and looks around carefully* Alright, listen close now. There's a man... Marcus Kane. Folks call him 'The Distiller.' He runs the REAL moonshine operations 'round these parts. Shacks, distribution, protection... the whole damn empire.
rcodialog13_text: *grins* Dangerous? Sure. But profitable. You've proven yourself with them stills and recipes. Kane's always lookin' for skilled brewers to run his shacks. Course, he takes a cut... 15% of everything you make. But with a shack, you're brewin' VOLUME, friend. Real money.
rcodialog14_text: *pulls out a worn map and marks a spot* Here. He operates outta this location. Tell him Old Coot sent ya, and that you're a proven brewer. He'll take care of the rest. Just remember... once you're in Kane's business, you play by his rules. Understood?
rcodialog15_text: *looks you up and down carefully* So... you're the one Old Coot's been talkin' about. Says you got a talent for brewin'. That true, or is the old man just drunk again?
rcodialog16_text: *lights a cigar* Name's Marcus Kane. I control every drop of illegal moonshine that flows through these territories. The shacks, the distribution, the protection... all mine. And I'm always lookin' for SKILLED operators.
rcodialog17_text: *leans forward* Here's the deal. I got moonshine shacks across the territory. You RENT one for 3 days, brew in VOLUME, sell to my network. You keep 85%% of profits. I take my 15%% cut for protection, distribution, and keepin' the law off your back. Fair?
rcodialog17_button2: I need to think about it.
rcodialog18_text: *nods* Back again. How's business at your shack? Remember, 15% of your profits come to me. It's the price of protection. What do you need?
